extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won the Paris-Nice Stage 1 sprint from deep in the bunch, emerging from a box-in behind LAPORTE and passing ACKERMANN after diving left then center.
tactical_patterns: GILBERT Philippe's pre-planned dual attack with teammate fractured peloton early, forcing defensive reactions before intermediate sprint point.
BENNETT Sam deliberately sprinted on brake hoods for power optimization, replicating Tour de France 2020 technique under pressure.
Late crash 1km out reshuffled sprint trains completely; LUTSENKO Alexey held up critically but avoided fall itself.
ACKERMANN Pascal's leadout man positioned him perfectly, but premature kick from 400m allowed fresher riders through on superior trajectories.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: Won from terrible position boxed behind LAPORTE, showing superior acceleration reserve and tactical sprint routing instincts.
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: Initiated coordinated twin attack with teammate, demonstrating pre-race planning despite being oldest rider at 38 years old.
rider_signals.LAWLESS Chris: negative: Completely cracked on minor climb during breakaway formation, surprising weakness for rider targeting sprint leadout duties.
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Faded to sixth despite perfect leadout positioning, suggests either premature sprint initiation or insufficient final 200m top-end power.
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: negative: Trapped on barriers right side in final 300m, tactical positioning error cost potential stage win despite strong form.
rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: negative: Held up by late crash losing sprint contention, positioning too far back with 1km remaining for sprinter ambitions.
